Name: Orano
Description:
Opérateur international reconnu dans le domaine des matières nucléaires, Orano apporte des solutions aux défis actuels et futurs, dans l’énergie et la santé. Son expertise ainsi que sa maîtrise des technologies de pointe permettent à Orano de proposer à ses clients des produits et services à forte valeur ajoutée sur l’ensemble du cycle du combustible.
Grâce à leurs compétences, leur exigence en matière de sûreté et de sécurité et leur recherche constante d’innovation, l’ensemble des 17 000 collaborateurs du groupe s'engage pour développer des savoir-faire de transformation et de maîtrise des matières nucléaires, pour le climat, pour la santé et pour un monde économe en ressources, aujourd’hui et demain. 
Orano, donnons toute sa valeur au nucléaire.
______________________________________________________________________________
As a recognized international operator in the field of nuclear materials, Orano delivers solutions to address present and future global energy and health challenges.
Its expertise and mastery of cutting-edge technologies enable Orano to offer its customers high value-added products and services throughout the entire fuel cycle.
Every day, the Orano group’s 17,000 employees draw on their skills, unwavering dedication to safety and constant quest for innovation, with the commitment to develop know-how in the transformation and control of nuclear materials, for the climate and for a healthy and resource-efficient world, now and tomorrow.
Orano, giving nuclear energy its full value.

Name: GE Vernova Hitachi Nuclear Energy
Description: GE Vernova Hitachi Nuclear Energy is a world-leading provider of advanced reactors, fuel and nuclear services. Our BWRX-300 small modular reactor is the first SMR under construction in the Western world.

Name: TerraPower
Description:
TerraPower is a leading nuclear innovation company that strives to improve the world through nuclear energy and science. TerraPower’s innovators create technologies to provide safe, affordable and abundant carbon-free energy. They devise ways to use heat and electricity to drive economic growth while decarbonizing industry. And they develop processes to extract radioisotopes for medical use in lifesaving cancer treatments.

We’re proud of our culture of mission-driven innovation. Our employees develop clean, safe energy to address climate change and global poverty, and drive medical advances for human health applications. We attract exceptional people and encourage them to confront big challenges with bold creativity. We drive hard for progress while keeping our values at the forefront.

TerraPower was founded by Bill Gates and a group of like-minded visionaries that decided the private sector needed to take action in developing advanced nuclear energy to meet growing electricity needs, mitigate climate change and lift billions out of poverty. Advanced reactors and other isotopic applications are now possible with technology and enhanced computing capabilities that were unimaginable a few decades ago. At TerraPower, we are innovating in nuclear to improve the lives of people everywhere and to build the clean energy of tomorrow – today.

TerraPower is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We do not discriminate in hiring on the basis of sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, color, religious creed, national origin, physical or mental disability, protected Veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local law. In addition, as a federal contractor, TerraPower has instituted an Affirmative Action Plan (AAP) in an effort to proactively recruit, hire, and promote women, minorities, disabled persons and veterans.

Name: GE Hitachi Nuclear Energy
Description: GE Vernova's nuclear business, GE Hitachi, is an alliance created by GE Vernova and Hitachi to serve the global nuclear industry, and is a world-leading provider of advanced reactors, fuel and nuclear services.

Name: World Nuclear News
Description:
On a daily basis World Nuclear News (WNN) provides free and accurate public information on the subject of nuclear power.  

The goal of WNN is to use plain English to place comprehensive coverage of nuclear power in context using background information, expert commentary and links to relevant authoritative sources. Because they are researched from original sources and written in-house by experienced journalists, WNN stories may be freely reproduced under certain licence conditions. 

The WNN service is supported administratively and with technical advice by the World Nuclear Association and is based within its London Secretariat. It draws on the World Nuclear Association's global network of contacts in industry, academia, research institutes and intergovernmental agencies that include key personnel in enterprises, which account for virtually all of the world's uranium mining, nuclear fuel manufacture, equipment production and nuclear power generation.

All WNN stories are available on this free-access website, which is updated every working day (observing UK public holidays).

Story headlines and summaries are compiled in the WNN Daily email and, on Tuesdays, in the WNN Weekly email. Both of these are available free by joining our mailing list - visit our website to find out more.

Readers can discuss stories on WNN's Facebook page, while the headlines of every story are carried on an RSS feed and through WNN's Twitter account, @W_Nuclear_News.

Name: newcleo
Description:
Since launching in 2021 newcleo has quickly established itself as innovator in the field of nuclear energy. newcleo is working to design, build, and operate Gen-IV Advanced Modular Reactors (AMRs) that are cooled by liquid lead and fuelled by reprocessed nuclear waste.

Through an innovative combination of existing and proven technologies, and by reviving a nuclear industry model based on the manufacture and multi-recycling of Mixed Oxide fuel, newcleo aims to close the nuclear fuel cycle while safely producing clean, affordable, and practically inexhaustible energy required for low carbon economies. 

With a EUR 50m group turnover in 2024, more than EUR 537m of private funding and over 90 partnerships and collaborations across the nuclear industry, the growth of the newcleo group is supported through the targeted acquisition of key companies with strong capabilities in nuclear engineering, manufacturing, and waste management. 

Through its workforce of over 900 highly qualified employees across France, the UK, Italy, Switzerland and Slovakia, newcleo is not only developing and delivering the skills and services required for the group’s own ambitious project timelines, but also supporting the development of Small Modular Reactor supply chains in Europe and beyond.

Name: X-energy
Description:
X-energy is delivering the next generation of carbon-free energy by reinventing nuclear power.

We believe in leaving a cleaner planet for our children and grandchildren, while continuing to provide sufficient and affordable power to sustain growing populations around the world. That is why we have reinvented nuclear power, with a unique combination of proven technologies and new innovations, to meet our current and future needs.  
 
X-energy manufactures fuel that seals uranium particles in a protective coating, which makes meltdown impossible and retains the waste inside forever. We also design plants that unlock the fuel’s potential in a process that’s as clean as wind or solar. When combined, the result is reliable carbon-free baseload power, produced more safely and affordable than ever before and available anywhere, at any time.

Name: World Nuclear Association
Description:
World Nuclear Association’s mission is to facilitate the growth of the nuclear sector by connecting players across the value chain, representing the industry’s position in key world forums, and providing authoritative information and influencing key audiences.

- We connect leaders and experts across all aspects of the nuclear industry to enable them to collaborate, conduct research, develop best practices and shape joint positions.
- We represent the nuclear industry in key global forums that influence the development, deployment and operation of nuclear energy.
- We inform key audiences on nuclear energy in an authoritative, comprehensive and accessible manner.
- We influence decision-makers, organizations and media to position nuclear energy as an essential contributor to sustainable development.

Name: Orano DS
Description: Orano DS fait partie du Groupe Orano, le fournisseur de référence du cycle du combustible nucléaire.  Ses 5000 salariés s’impliquent au quotidien auprès des  exploitants nucléaires français et internationaux pour leur apporter expertise et solutions éprouvées dans les métiers de services aux exploitants nucléaires (logistique industrielle, maintenance spécialisée et sécurité radiologique), de   l’assainissement-démantèlement et de la gestion de déchets.

Name: Akkuyu Nükleer
Description:
JSC AKKUYU NÜKLEER is a subsidiary of Russian State Atomic Energy Corporation Rosatom established under the terms of the Intergovernmental Agreement of May 12, 2010 between the Government of the Russian Federation and the Government of the Republic of Turkey on cooperation in the construction and operation of the nuclear power plant at the Akkuyu site in the Republic of Turkey.  

Akkuyu NPP is the ﬁrst nuclear power plant in Turkey. The NPP will include four power units with VVER-1200 reactors of III+ generation, equipped with advanced safety systems. 
The total installed capacity of the NPP is 4,800 MW. Designed lifetime of each unit is 60 years with the possibility of extension for another 20 years. 

When four power units of the Akkuyu NPP are put into operation, they will produce about 35 billion kWh of electricity per year. Akkuyu NPP will meet up to 10% of Turkey’s power demand. 

The New Energy of a Strong Turkey

Name: The Nuclear Company
Description: The Nuclear Company is leading the modernization of nuclear construction to deploy fleet-scale nuclear power across America. By integrating proven reactor technologies with digital innovation and economies of scale, the company aims to reduce costs and shorten deployment timelines. The Nuclear Company is committed to delivering safe baseload electricity at the lowest cost, while catalyzing the nuclear industry toward rapid development in America and globally.

Name: Polskie Elektrownie Jądrowe
Description:
We are Polskie Elektrownie Jądrowe Sp. z o. o. [Eng. – ‘Polish Nuclear Power Plants’] and we carry out tasks aimed at strengthening Poland's energy security.

We believe that nuclear energy is the key to providing Poland with a significant amount of safe, stable, and environmentally friendly electricity generated with respect for the environment. That is why our plan is to launch Poland's first nuclear power plant.

We are responsible for:

- Preparing the entire investment process and then acting as the investor in the project to build the first nuclear power plant in Poland. We will also be responsible for constructing further nuclear blocks (with a total installed capacity of around 6 GWe to around 9 GWe) based on proven large-scale Generation III(+) pressurised water nuclear reactors and, potentially, for their future operation.
- Providing support for governmental administration in activities aimed at achieving the objectives of the Polish Nuclear Power Programme.
- Providing reliable information on nuclear energy and building public support for its development,

as well as other aspects of the Polish Nuclear Power Programme.

We are committed to fulfilling our mission in a responsible manner, which is why, at the beginning, more than 90 potential sites for the first nuclear power plant in Poland had been analysed with the participation of experts in various fields. Then, based on the results of those analyses, the two best sites were selected, and in recent years we have carried out very detailed location and environmental studies in these sites. The preferred location for a nuclear power plant in Poland will be indicated based on the results of these studies.

Our owner is the State Treasury and day-to-day operations are supervised by the Government Plenipotentiary for Strategic Energy Infrastructure.

The privacy information for users of our company's profile is available at: https://ppej.pl/Polityka-prywatnosci

Name: SPIE Nucléaire
Description:
SPIE Nucléaire est un partenaire majeur de la filière, intervenant dans la construction, la maintenance, les travaux sur sites en exploitation et le démantèlement des installations nucléaires à travers ses métiers du génie électrique, mécanique, tuyauterie / soudage, climatique et nucléaire. Nous accompagnons depuis plus de 60 ans les grands donneurs d’ordres dans la mutation profonde de l’Industrie du Nucléaire, en s’adaptant et en faisant preuve d’une excellence opérationnelle notamment dans les domaines de la sécurité et de la sûreté où elle répond aux plus grandes exigences de ses clients.

SPIE Nucléaire & ses filiales vous accompagnent dans l’optimisation
de la sûreté et de la disponibilité des installations nucléaires à travers
ses 4 domaines d’activités stratégiques et ses 4 métiers.

Nos 1 900 collaborateurs interviennent sur 45 sites et implantations1
partout en France pour s’inscrire durablement du côté de la solution
de la transition énergétique dans une filière nucléaire d’avenir.

Name: Eletronuclear
Description:
Criada em 1997, a Eletronuclear é a única empresa no Brasil a produzir eletricidade a partir de fonte nuclear. Atualmente, opera as usinas Angra 1 e Angra 2 para gerar eletricidade e movimentar a vida de milhões de brasileiros.
Há mais de 30 anos, a Eletronuclear diversifica o abastecimento de eletricidade no Brasil, contribuindo para o desenvolvimento do país. Os potentes geradores das usinas Angra 1 (640MW) e Angra 2 (1.350MW) produzem o equivalente a 40% de toda a energia consumida no Estado do Rio de Janeiro.

As usinas nucleares geram energia a partir do calor liberado pela fissão do núcleo do átomo do urânio. Durante esse processo não são produzidos gases poluentes e causadores do efeito estufa.

Para garantir que a operação das usinas nucleares não traga impactos significativos ao meio ambiente, a Eletronuclear desenvolve um permanente e rigoroso programa de monitoração tanto em ambiente marinho quanto terrestre. Além disso, a empresa investe em programas de educação ambiental e de preservação. 

Para a Eletronuclear, a segurança é um compromisso prioritário e vai muito além das grossas paredes de aço e concreto que cercam os reatores das usinas Angra 1 e Angra 2. Segurança nuclear é um processo contínuo que envolve treinamentos, programas de testes periódicos, procedimentos de trabalho, simulações e processos de avaliação interna e externa.

Hoje, com a experiência acumulada em todos os anos de operação e o domínio de altas tecnologias, a Eletronuclear pode se orgulhar de ter as usinas Angra 1 e Angra 2 classificadas entre as mais eficientes do planeta!

Name: Hadron Energy, Inc.
Description:
Hadron Energy specializes in Micro Modular Reactor (MMR) development, design, and research. Each unit produces 10 megawatts of electricity to power data center sites, industrial applications, and remote communities with 24/7 carbon free power.

Follow Hadron Energy for key insights in the energy industry and advancements in nuclear technology for the betterment of humanity.

Name: Radiant
Description: Radiant is building the world’s first mass-produced portable nuclear microreactors. The company’s first reactor, Kaleidos, is a 1 MW failsafe microreactor that can be transported anywhere power is needed. Founded in 2020, Radiant plans to test its first reactor in 2026, with initial customer deployments beginning in 2028.

Name: Nucleoeléctrica Argentina S.A.
Description:
Nucleoeléctrica Argentina S.A. (NASA) es una empresa generadora de energía eléctrica, operadora de las centrales nucleares argentinas y encargada de la gestión de los proyectos nucleares del país.

La compañía produce energía nucleoeléctrica mediante la operación de las centrales Atucha I, Atucha II y Embalse. La potencia instalada total de sus tres plantas es de 1763 MW.

Name: NUWARD
Description:
The NUWARD SMR is targeting to replace old high CO2-emitting coal, fuel, oil and gas plants around the world as well as offering the capability of supporting other usages such as hydrogen production, urban and district heating or desalination. 

Based on proven PWR technology, NUWARD SMR will be able to produce up to 400 MWe and 115 MWth. Led by the NUWARD company, the project relies on the skilled and valued contributions of its European partners.

Name: Terrestrial Energy
Description: Terrestrial Energy is a developer of Generation IV nuclear plants that use its proprietary Integral Molten Salt Reactor (IMSR) technology. IMSR technology captures the full transformative operating benefits of molten salt reactor technology in a plant design that represents true innovation in cost reduction, versatility and functionality of nuclear energy supply. IMSR plants will supply zero-carbon, reliable, dispatchable, low-cost high-temperature industrial heat and electricity for a dual-use energy role relevant to many industrial applications, such as chemical synthesis and desalination. In so doing, they extend the application of nuclear energy far beyond electric power markets. IMSR plants have the potential to make substantial contributions to industrial competitiveness, energy security, and economic growth. Their deployment will support rapid global decarbonization of the primary energy system across a broad spectrum. Terrestrial Energy uses an innovative design, and proven and demonstrated molten salt reactor technology, which offers a unique set of operating characteristics that deliver high and compelling commercial potential. Terrestrial Energy is engaged with regulators, suppliers and industrial partners to build, license and commission the first IMSR power plants in the early 2030s.

Name: Korea Hydro & Nuclear Power (KHNP)
Description:
KHNP(Korea Hydro & Nuclear Power) provides about 30 percent of South Korea's electricity supply power generation company.

It has a total installed capacity of more than 25,000MW from operating 25 nuclear power units, 28 hydropower units, 16 pumped-storage power units and a number of renewable energy facilities. 

KHNP is in the process of building 3 nuclear power plants (NPPs) domestically and has 2 additional units in the planning or preparation stage. 
Through close partnerships with local governments, the company is actively developing new and renewable energy sources, including small hydropower, photovoltaic and wind power, and it is also operating the world's largest fuel cell power plant with 58.8MW of capacity. 

In late 2009, South Korea secured its first commercial nuclear plant export contract from the United Arab Emirates, covering the delivery of four Advanced Power Reactor 1400 (APR 1400) units. 
Construction of the first APR 1400, which is acclaimed for its higher structural safety and improved economic feasibility, is slated to be completed by 2017. 

In addition to the UAE, KHNP is actively pursuing export of its NPPs to other nations.

In Asia, KHNP inked formal agreements last year with China National Nuclear Power, a state-owned NPP operator, and Kansai Electric Power of Japan for active technology, personnel and information exchange. 
KHNP has also been providing benchmarking training programmes to China's Qinshan NPP for the past 16 years.

To further reinforce its technological competitiveness, KHNP has unveiled the APR+, a 1,500MW-class large NPP developed solely using Korean technology. 

Last year, the standard design of the next-generation nuclear reactor was approved by South Korea's Nuclear Safety and Security Commission. The company hopes to diversify its export markets with the launching of the APR+.

Name: Saltfoss Energy
Description:
Saltfoss Energy is a scale-up company at the forefront of advanced and innovative nuclear reactor technology working to provide abundant, affordable, and sustainable energy available to all. 
We have grown to become the leading molten salt reactor start-up outside of North America with an international team from all over the world. We are actively developing the design for our Compact Molten Salt Reactor, alongside licensing and experimental validation activities of key safety and performance claims, and are currently building new experimental facilities.

Name: Last Energy
Description: Last Energy's mission is to decarbonize global energy production and increase access to clean, affordable power. We are a full-service developer of micro modular nuclear power plants with the goal of finally scaling nuclear by dramatically reducing the time and cost of construction.

Name: Nuclear Institute
Description: The Nuclear Institute is the UK professional membership organisation for everyone who works in nuclear. We maintain the standards of professionalism in the nuclear sector through our code of conduct and the Nuclear Delta. Of most importance to us is the education and training of nuclear professionals, as well as the understanding of the nuclear industry for the wider public.

Name: Teollisuuden Voima Oyj
Description: Teollisuuden Voima Oyj (TVO) contributes to the maintenance of sustainable development and the well-being of Finnish people by providing shareholders with cost price electricity produced in a safe, economical, and climate-friendly manner at the Olkiluoto nuclear power plant in Eurajoki.

Name: copenhagen atomics
Description:
Copenhagen Atomics pioneers thorium molten salt reactors, a groundbreaking solution that burns nuclear waste to generate abundant and cost-effective green energy. These compact and modular reactors, designed to fit the same area of a standard 40-foot shipping container, ensure scalability and easy deployment worldwide.

With a relentless focus on product development, Copenhagen Atomics has achieved groundbreaking milestones, including the creation of the world's first commercially available molten salt test loops and highly purified salts, catering to the needs of companies, universities, and national labs globally.

Our key milestone is to test our reactor at the Paul Scherrer Institute in Switzerland.

Name: NAAREA
Description:
Le but de NAAREA est de rendre compatible le développement continu des activités humaines avec la protection de l’héritage et du patrimoine de l’humanité que constitue le climat de notre planète.

Cette finalité dépend directement de l’énergie. L’atteinte des 17 Objectifs de Développement Durable de l’Agenda 2030 des Nations Unies est une nécessité absolue pour l’humanité (éradiquer la pauvreté, protéger la planète, garantir la prospérité).

Name: Canadians for CANDU
Description:
Canadians for CANDU supports a made-in-Canada solution to achieve net-zero. Our mission is to secure Canada’s clean energy future through the adoption of CANDU technology.

As we rapidly move toward a net-zero future driven by clean nuclear power, Canadian energy policy is at a crossroads. 

Will we seize a once-in-a-generation opportunity to take on a global leadership role with CANDU, the only Canadian-made and Canadian-owned nuclear technology on the market? Will Canada be a buyer and importer? Or a builder and exporter of world-class technology that supports a home-grown supply chain? Will we champion Canadian jobs, innovation, and economic growth, all while confronting the pressing challenges of climate change?

The choice is clear. It’s time to choose CANDU.

********

Les Canadiens pour CANDU militent en faveur d'une solution canadienne pour atteindre la carboneutralité. Notre mission est d'assurer l'avenir énergétique propre du Canada par l'adoption de la technologie CANDU.

Alors que nous nous dirigeons rapidement vers un avenir carboneutre grâce à l’énergie nucléaire propre, la politique énergétique du Canada est à la croisée des chemins.

Saurons-nous saisir une occasion unique d’assurer un leadership mondial avec le réacteur CANDU, la seule technologie nucléaire sur le marché qui est fabriquée au Canada et qui appartient à des intérêts canadiens ?

Le Canada sera-t-il un acheteur et un importateur ? Ou un constructeur et un exportateur d’une technologie de calibre mondiale et qui fait vivre une chaîne d’approvisionnement locale ?

Défendrons-nous l’emploi, l’innovation et la croissance économique au Canada, tout en relevant les défis pressants reliés au changement climatique ?

Le choix est clair. Il est temps de choisir CANDU.

Name: Sizewell C Consortium
Description:
As a Consortium, we are all different.  Our membership includes over 200 businesses, supply chain companies and trade unions from every walk of life, and every part of the country.

From scientists, to welders, builders, manufacturers, caterers, coders and more – we all stand ready to deliver one goal: Getting Sizewell C built for Britain.

Why we support Britain’s next nuclear power station:

Built by Britain: UK-based companies will receive 70% of the project’s construction value: that’s over £14 billion benefiting British businesses and supporting UK jobs and industry.

Jobs and skills across the UK: Through a nationwide supply-chain, Sizewell C will bring over 70,000 jobs to workers and businesses throughout the country, stimulating bottom-up economic growth.

Certainty for our ready and raring nuclear supply-chain: Sizewell C will bring national and constituency level jobs and contractors that will pioneer all forms of nuclear technology to secure Britain’s energy future could fall by the wayside.

Unleashing our industrial potential: Britain has long been a leader in energy infrastructure, but hope has faded towards our ability to build for ourselves – building Sizewell C can show that all to be wrong.

Name: Jimmy
Description: Jimmy conçoit et opère des générateurs thermiques atomiques pour décarboner massivement l'industrie

Name: Renaissance Fusion
Description: Nuclear fusion start-up in Europe making the stellarator simple, fusion electricity incredibly cheap, and High Temperature Superconductors (HTS) widely available.

Name: Nuclear Power Corporation of India Limited(NPCIL)-Official
Description: Nuclear Power Corporation of India Limited (NPCIL) is a Government of India Enterprise under the administrative control of Department of Atomic Energy (DAE).

Name: NANO Nuclear Energy Inc. (NASDAQ: NNE)
Description:
NANO Nuclear Energy Inc. (NASDAQ: NNE) NANO Nuclear Energy Inc. (NASDAQ: NNE) is a North American advanced technology-driven nuclear energy company seeking to become a commercially focused, diversified, and vertically integrated company across five business lines: (i) cutting edge portable and other microreactor technologies, (ii) nuclear fuel fabrication, (iii) nuclear fuel transportation, (iv) nuclear applications for space and (v) nuclear industry consulting services. NANO Nuclear believes it is the first portable nuclear microreactor company to be listed publicly in the U.S.

Led by a world-class nuclear engineering team, NANO Nuclear’s reactor products in development include its lead project, the patented KRONOS MMR Energy System, a stationary high-temperature gas-cooled reactor that is in construction permit pre-application engagement with the U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) in collaboration with University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign (U. of I.), ZEUS, a solid core battery reactor, and the space focused, portable LOKI MMR, each representing advanced developments in clean energy solutions that are modular, on-demand capable, advanced nuclear microreactors.

Advanced Fuel Transportation Inc. (AFT), a NANO Nuclear subsidiary, is led by former executives from the largest transportation company in the world aiming to build a North American transportation company that will provide commercial quantities of HALEU fuel to small modular reactors, microreactor companies, national laboratories, military, and DOE programs.

HALEU Energy Fuel Inc. (HEF), a NANO Nuclear subsidiary, is focusing on the future development of a domestic source for a High-Assay, Low-Enriched Uranium (HALEU) fuel fabrication pipeline for NANO Nuclear’s own microreactors as well as the broader advanced nuclear reactor industry.

NANO Nuclear Space Inc. (NNS), is exploring the potential commercial applications of NANO Nuclear’s developing micro nuclear reactor technology in space.

Name: BWXT Canada
Description:
BWXT Canada Ltd. (BWXT Canada) has over 60 years of expertise and experience in the design, manufacturing, commissioning and service of nuclear power generation equipment. This includes CANDU and Pressurized Water Reactor steam generators, nuclear fuel and fuel components, critical plant components, parts and related plant services. Headquartered in Cambridge, Ontario, BWXT Canada has approximately 1290 employees at locations in Cambridge, Peterborough, Port Elgin, Pickering, Toronto,  Arnprior, and Oakville, Ontario. 

As a supplier of over 305 CANDU and pressurized water reactor steam generators worldwide, as well as other major plant components and fuel, we have the knowledge and expertise to develop innovative solutions for all areas of plant operations from the nuclear reactor itself to conventional systems, replacement reactor vessel closure heads, pressurizers, primary piping, critical heat exchangers, and spent fuel storage.

Name: Aalo Atomics
Description: Energy without limits, for everyone.

Name: INB
Description:
Nosso negócio é o urânio - atuamos na cadeia produtiva do minério, o "ciclo do combustível nuclear", que inclui a mineração, o beneficiamento, o enriquecimento e a fabricação de pastilhas e do combustível que abastece as usinas nucleares brasileiras.

Vinculados ao Ministério de Minas e Energia, exercemos, em nome da União, o monopólio da produção e comercialização de materiais nucleares. Também atuamos na execução de serviços de engenharia do combustível e na produção de componentes dos elementos combustíveis.

Os processos industriais e os programas de controle ambiental e segurança radiológica que desenvolvemos garantem a qualidade de nossos produtos e asseguram a preservação do meio ambiente, da saúde dos trabalhadores e das populações, especialmente aquelas que habitam nas áreas de influência de nossas unidades.

As atividades da INB são licenciadas e fiscalizadas pela Comissão Nacional de Energia Nuclear (CNEN) e pelo Instituto Brasileiro do Meio Ambiente e dos Recursos Naturais Renováveis (IBAMA). A Agência Internacional de Energia Atômica (AIEA), órgão vinculado à Organização das Nações Unidas (ONU), supervisiona nossa produção, realizando inspeções regulares à Fábrica de Combustível Nuclear.
Location: Rio de Janeiro, Rio de Janeiro

Name: ETC Nederland
Description:
ETC is an innovative technology company committed to the safe, competitive, and sustainable delivery of enrichment technology services and energy solutions to our customers.

Our expertise lies in the design and delivery of high quality manufacturing, engineering and technology solutions for the needs of today's and future global energy market. Thanks to many years of experience in the nuclear sector, ETC's business activities focus on two areas:

1.	Producing ultra-gas centrifuges, the most efficient technology for producing enriched uranium as a fuel for nuclear energy production.
2.	Supplying advanced high-tech solutions, manufacturing high-tech products and providing services such as design engineering for customers in the aerospace industry, semiconductor and textiles, packaging & printing industry. 

Clean energy
At ETC, we believe that reducing CO2 emissions is important for the environment. We support our customers in their efforts to achieve a cleaner environment by applying our technical knowledge in R&D, engineering and manufacturing, which has been accumulated over decades.

Name: nucleareurope
Description: Nucleareurope is the Brussels-based trade association for the nuclear energy industry in Europe. Its main purpose is to promote the use of nuclear energy in Europe by representing the interests of this important and multi-faceted industrial sector.

Name: Ansaldo Nuclear
Description:
Ansaldo Nuclear is one of the largest independent turnkey providers of Nuclear Engineering, Manufacturing & Services in the UK, with over 60 years of UK & international experience.

Our organisation combines the leading design & manufacturing capabilities of Ansaldo Nuclear Ltd (UK) and the extensive knowledge of Ansaldo Nucleare SpA (Italy).

We provide tailored design, engineering, manufacturing, assembly, testing, commissioning, on-site installation and integrated logistics services for the following nuclear markets:

• Nuclear New Builds
• Decommissioning and nuclear waste management
• Fusion
• Plant operation assistance
• Defence (UK only)

With offices throughout the globe, we have supplied critical equipment and solutions to every nuclear licensed site in the UK, plus a number of international markets. This broad-based presence allows us to quickly react to our customers’ needs and offer a richer value proposition.

That's why we're proud to call ourselves a one-stop shop for nuclear power solutions.

Name: THORIZON
Description:
While battling climate change, and to ensure our energy future, we should use every available source of clean energy to its fullest. At Thorizon we take the potential of nuclear energy to the next level. There still is a tremendous amount of energy available in existing nuclear waste. We have developed a technology to use it.

The Thorizon One, a modular molten salt reactor that transforms nuclear waste in carbon-free energy, is safe by design. Its core consists of multiple fully enclosed ‘cartridges’, which can be replaced to incorporate new innovations along the way. We reduce the amount of long-lived radioactive waste and generate 250MW of clean energy in one go. Safe, smart, and circular.

Name: Blykalla
Description:
Blykalla is on a mission to change the energy future of the world, by providing green, safe, baseload energy through next generation small modular lead-cooled reactors. 

Blykalla is a Swedish deep-tech company  founded in 2013 by Janne Wallenius, Peter Szakalos and Jesper Ejenstam as a spin-off from the Royal Institute of Technology in Stockholm, where Janne Wallenius carried out research on design and safety analysis on lead-cooled reactor systems since 1996. 

We develop and build lead-cooled small modular reactors (SMRs) to deliver safe, scalable and carbon-free baseload power supply. Thanks to lead-cooling, we achieve high safety in a very compact format, allowing for a simple and cost effective design that can be mass produced. Our unique reactor design will provide safe and green baseload energy that is a perfect complement to solar and wind power.

Name: ORLEN Synthos Green Energy
Description:

Name: Centrus Energy Corp.
Description: Centrus Energy is a trusted supplier of nuclear fuel and services for the nuclear power industry. Centrus provides value to its utility customers through the reliability and diversity of its supply sources – helping them meet the growing need for clean, affordable, carbon-free electricity. Since 1998, the Company has provided its utility customers with more than 1,850 reactor years of fuel, which is equivalent to 7 billion tons of coal. With world-class technical and engineering capabilities, Centrus is also advancing the next generation of centrifuge technologies so that America can restore its domestic uranium enrichment capability in the future. Find out more at www.centrusenergy.com.

Name: Moltex Energy
Description:
Moltex Energy is developing a next generation nuclear reactor that can produce clean electricity at a lower cost than fossil fuels. The technology is based on molten salt fuel in conventional fuel assemblies. Existing stockpiles of spent nuclear fuel can be used as the fuel source and the electricity produced is entirely flexible. 
Nuclear can now be considered as a peaking plant, not only baseload. 
Partnered with New Brunswick Power to deploy the first facilities at Point Lepreau site, Moltex has operations in Canada, the UK, and the USA.

Name: Foro Nuclear
Description:
At Foro Nuclear we defend the interests of the Spanish nuclear sector and support the continuity of Spanish nuclear power plants, which produce a constant and stable source of electricity and contribute to over 20% of the electricity consumed in Spain. 

We believe that nuclear power is essential to the energy transition, as well as a key element in reducing polluting emissions since nuclear technology is free of CO2. 

Additionally, the  competitive, technological and cutting-edge nuclear industry generates wealth and employment. 

You may also follow us on our website at www.foronuclear.org in X as @Foronuclear, in Facebook at https://www.facebook.com/foronuclear and in Instagram as @Foronuclear

Name: Paragon Energy Solutions
Description:
Paragon tackles the most difficult challenges of the nuclear industry—those faced by the current operating fleet as well as the next generation of advanced reactors. Now part of Mirion Technologies, a worldwide leader in radiation safety, detection, and measurement, Paragon combines deep nuclear expertise with the strength and global reach of a trusted industry leader.

For more than three decades, Paragon has been dedicated to quality, safety, reliability, and carbon-free energy across the nuclear sector. We produce and test mechanical, electrical, and I&C mission-critical equipment that meets the industry’s most demanding nuclear standards. Through our industry-leading repair and reverse-engineering services—and through PeAks, our web-based marketplace with a robust parts inventory—we provide proven solutions to obsolescence and parts availability challenges.

When reactor-specific needs arise, our innovative team leverages advanced technology and superior technical expertise to develop comprehensive, custom solutions from the ground up. Trusted by our customers, Paragon supplies every operating nuclear plant in the United States and Canada, along with a growing number of facilities.

Paragon delivers effective, efficient solutions that reduce costs and enhance performance. Backed by Mirion Technologies, our unparalleled services address today’s industry challenges while enabling the opportunities of tomorrow’s advanced reactors.

Our energy future is nuclear, and Paragon is proud to power it.

Name: Urenco Capenhurst

With a total workforce of over 1,000 people based at Capenhurst, the Company is an international supplier of enrichment services and fuel cycle products with sustainability at the core of its business. Description:

Stellaria can regenerates its liquid salty fissile fuel on the go, without having to unload it. 
Nuclear fission reinvented. Power for centuries without more mines. 
Safety and sustainability fully fixed.

Name: Society of Professional Engineers and Associates
Description:
The Society of Professional Engineers and Associates (SPEA) is an independent union representing engineers, scientists, technicians, technologists, skilled tradespersons, drafters, designers and other specialists employed by Candu Energy Inc. (formerly Atomic Energy of Canada Limited). SPEA members play an integral role in new nuclear reactor builds. They have helped design and build new reactors in South Korea, China, and Romania. They have also participated in refurbishing reactors in Ontario (Bruce Power) and, Korea (Wolsong), Quebec (Gentilly-2), New Brunswick (Pt. Lepreau) and Argentina (Embalse).

Formed in 1974, SPEA is one of the oldest professional unions in Canada. Collectively we represent most of Canada's nuclear power reactor design expertise. SPEA represents all technical employees at Candu, in three separate bargaining units (the "SE" bargaining unit is comprised of scientists, engineers and other professionals, the "TT" bargaining unit consists of technicians, technologists and trades, and the “PD” bargaining unit consists of drafters and designers). Each bargaining unit has a separate collective agreement with the same employer.

Name: iThEC
Description:
iThEC (international Thorium Energy Committee) is a Swiss-based association of physicists, engineers, politicians and other like-minded people aiming at developing thorium energy systems, with a focus on Accelerator-Driven Systems, Carlo Rubbia’s brainchild, with the capacity of destroying nuclear waste.

iThEC is a legal entity under art. 60 of the Swiss civil law. It has been formally created in Geneva on September 12th, 2012, prior to the ThEC13 meeting held at CERN. It holds a General Assembly meeting annually in the Geneva area, and keeps its members informed through several additional informal lunches where everyone is invited to participate. These lunches are usually held at CERN. New information and progress of the project are added on our website in the member’s area.

Every interested person, and willing to contribute to iThEC’s goals, is welcome: scientists, legal and finance people, politicians…

Name: NuCon Company
Description:
NuCon Company (NuCon) is a US-based business established in Texas in April 2001 as a nuclear consulting business. NuCon re-registered in IL in 2025. Today, the company is still actively developing projects in nuclear energy, particularly in the transportation of small nuclear reactors. Since 2005, NuCon has been independently developing three conceptual and pre-conceptual projects for modern (advanced) Small Modular Reactors (SMR):
•	Light-Water SMR (LW-SMR) maritime project based on full Natural Circulation (NC) and integral Reactor Vessel (RV) design;
•	Two Fast Liquid Metal SMR (FLM-SMR) projects with two different coolants (Pb-Bi and Na-Pb) and original fuel elements (FE) geometry, as well as a scalable semi-block RV design.
All these three concepts involve creating a standardized, compact, and modular Nuclear Steam Supply Block (NSSB) with high inherent nuclear safety and physical security. 
The Principal Investigator (PI) possesses extensive theoretical and practical knowledge of most modern naval LW- and FLM-SMR projects, as well as years of practical experience and education in the maritime nuclear power propulsion plant (NPPP) field. It helps bring significant scientific and technical information on the latest developments and innovations to the project. 
The NuCon is uniquely positioned to support the development of novel LW- and/or FLM-SMR projects. The company is fully independent and has never used the support of any state institutions or external investors; it is completely free from any financial or Intellectual Property (IP) obligations.
In addition, we are developing a specialized and unique analytical tool for the process approach of integrated nuclear safety analysis (INSA), which can be implemented as a fundamental principle for engineering and design activities in the development of new and advanced nuclear reactor projects.

• Arbeitsplatz- und Dosisüberwachung: Durchführung von radiologischen Messungen, Prüfung der Schutzausrüstung und Dokumentation unter Einhaltung der Strahlenschutzverordnung (StrlSchV).

• Ingenieur- und Projektleistungen: Planung, Bewertung und technische Begleitung kerntechnischer Projekte. Organisation, Koordination und Leitung von Revisions- und Rückbauvorhaben.

 Sicherheits- und Qualitätsmanagement: Erstellung und Prüfung von Strahlenschutzkonzepten, Freimessstrategien und Sicherheitsdokumentationen.

• Behörden- und Genehmigungsmanagement: Unterstützung bei Anträgen, Meldungen und Nachweisen gegenüber Aufsichtsbehörden.

Unsere Mitarbeiter verfügen über langjährige Erfahrung in kerntechnischen Anlagen, Zwischenlagern und Rückbauprojekten. Wir vereinen technisches Know-how, präzises Projektmanagement und höchste Standards in Sicherheit und Qualität.


Wir suchen ständig Verstärkung!

Wir bieten spannende Einsatzmöglichkeiten in Deutschland und umliegendem Ausland für Strahlenschutzwerker, Strahlenschutzfachkräfte, Ingenieure sowie motivierte Quereinsteiger, die sich im Strahlenschutz qualifizieren möchten.

Wir legen Wert auf Fachkompetenz, Zuverlässigkeit und Verantwortungsbewusstsein – und bieten langfristige Perspektiven in einem technisch anspruchsvollen Umfeld.

Name: Gamma Initiative
Description: The Gamma Initiative is a youth-led organization dedicated to empowering the next generation of leaders in nuclear energy and climate action by removing barriers that keep young people from engaging with the field and showing how nuclear power fits into a sustainable, secure energy future. We provide education through accessible resources, workshops, and events; advocacy by giving young voices a platform to challenge misconceptions about nuclear power; networking to connect students with industry professionals and organizations; and opportunities through projects, collaborations, and leadership roles that prepare high school and university students to participate in the clean energy transition. Unlike conventional nuclear systems that rely on 100-year-old steam turbine designs, our system operates at higher temperatures and directly drives a gas turbine. This results in a simpler, safer, and more cost-effective solution—one that slashes capital costs by 3–5x and makes small-scale nuclear economically viable for the first time.

Our reactor is passively safe, air-cooled, and built from widely available materials—avoiding the cost and complexity of graphite or high-pressure systems. This leap forward in nuclear engineering was developed in partnership with the University of Manchester and BAE Systems.

Nuclear Turbines is uniquely positioned to deliver flexible, dispatchable baseload power to meet the world’s growing energy demands—from industrial users and data centres to remote grids.

Generating reliable, carbon-free power is only part of stewarding the Earth's resources. Small modular reactor (SMR) technology uses less land, conserves water resources, and prioritizes safety to balance today's energy demands with the needs of our planet. Small modular reactors (SMRs) are advanced nuclear reactors that have a power capacity of up to 300 MW(e) unit, which are designed to be manufactured off-site and then delivered to a location for final assembly.

SMRs are poised to contribute to meeting rising global demand for small-scale, zero-carbon power generation, while also providing advantages such as lower capital costs, flexibility in placement, and shorter construction times. They can easily be part of any clean energy solution mix for a country or region needing to transition its energy sector away from fossil sources.

Our work spans:
• Enterprise and nuclear safety culture and maintenance discipline
• Hiring fairness and public‑sector compliance
• Supplier Code of Conduct compliance and enforcement
• FOI‑driven process verification
• Governance structures under stress

We are not adversarial. We are pro-nuclear, structured, disciplined, and committed to helping build public trust through credible, documented, and non‑speculative analysis.

Name: SEAKNOT-EU Project
Description:
The SEAKNOT (SEvere Accident Research and Knowledge ManagemenT for LWRS) project, funded by Horizon Europe / EURATOM2027 programme (GA 101060327), is led by CIEMAT (Spain), and constituted by 17 well-known organisations from 10 countries: UPM (Spain); IRSN, CEA and LGi (France); ENEA and UNIPI (Italy), KIT, BECKER, FZJ Technologies and FRAMATOME GmbH (Germany); JSI (Slovenia); KTH (Sweden); UJV (Czech Republic); VTT (Finland); TRACTEBEL (Belgium); PSI (Switzerland, as associated partner). 
The four-year project aim is two-fold: to shape up a roadmap for severe accident research in the coming 10 years to achieve a substantial reduction of remaining uncertainties and a measurable progress in the practical elimination of radiological consequences; and, no less important, to efficiently manage the knowledge and knowhow transfer to the young generation of nuclear scientists and engineers. The first purpose would require: to build a Phenomena Identification and Ranking Table; to develop a relational database directory to help identifying validation experiments in each of the areas that a severe accident is traditionally split into, and to map the European experimental platforms that would be necessary to address the issues considered worth by means of networking in a sort of experimental platforms. The second purpose will be targeted with initiatives such as the Severe Accident Phenomenlology (SAP) course, the ERMSAR (European Review Meeting on Severe Accident Research), a second edition of the Severe Accident book issued by SARNET (Severe Accident Research NETwork) more than 10 years ago; and, a good number of dissemination activities. All these activities and more will be supported by the ambitious mobility programme within the project.

Name: ThorAtom
Description:
Birleşmiş Milletler bünyesindeki Hükûmetlerarası İklim Değişikliği Paneli (IPCC) sonuçları ve 2022 Şubat’ında Konya’da gerçekleştirilen Türkiye I. İklim Şûrası kararları gerek Türkiye gerekse Dünya için nasıl bir iklim krizi tehdidi altında olduğumuzu gözler önüne sermektedir. ThorAtom, sanayi devrimiyle birlikte dünya atmosferinde özellikle enerji kullanımından artan “sera etkili gazların” küresel iklimde ağır sonuçlar getirmeye başladığının farkında olarak, yeşil bir dünya için çalışmaya kararlıdır. Bu bağlamda enerji insanlık ve gezegenimiz için bir gelecek meselesi olarak ortaya çıkmaktadır. Çevre dostu ve yüksek güvenliğe sahip yeni nesil nükleer reaktörler, sadece iklim değişikliğinin yol açacağı zararlı etkileri azaltmakla kalmayıp aynı zamanda Türkiye’nin enerji bağımsızlığına büyük ölçüde katkıda bulunacaktır.

IV. Nesil Nükleer reaktörler içinde ön plana çıkan “Ergimiş Tuz Reaktörü” nü ülkemizin ihtiyaçlarına göre geliştirmek, tasarlamak ve üretmektir. Yakıt olarak uranyumun yanı sıra Türkiye’nin büyük rezervlere sahip olduğu toryumu kullanmak, ThorAtom AŞ’nin ana ilkelerinden biridir. Bunun yanı sıra Ergimiş Tuz Reaktörünün (ETR) sağlamış olduğu hem ısı hem de elektrik enerjisi ETR’nin uygulama alanlarını önemli derecede arttırmaktadır. ETR enerjisinden yararlanarak yeşil hidrojen ve yeşil amonyak üretimi, deniz suyundan tatlı su üretimi, soğuk bölgelerde seracılık, yüzer platformlarda itki gücü olarak kullanılması, ThorAtom AŞ’nin ilgi alanlarına girmektedir.

ThorAtom AŞ; kaynakları verimli olarak kullanan, zararlı salımları olmayan çevre bakımından yenilikçi teknolojileri geliştirme ve gerçekleştirme öncülüğünü üstlenmiş ilk Türk özel girişimidir. Nükleer enerji üretimi iklim kriziyle mücadelede en verimli ve en ekonomik seçenek olarak ortaya çıkmaktadır. Türkiye için IV. Nesil sürdürülebilir nükleer teknolojilerin tercih edilmesi ülkemize yeni ufuklar açacak niteliktedir.

Name: ETC UK
Description:
ETC is an international high-tech company whose main business is the development, manufacturing and installation of gas centrifuges for uranium enrichment. ETC also delivers aluminium pipework solutions to its customers and is a provider of project management, plant design and engineering services. This makes ETC a highly specialized supplier within the #nuclear power industry, making a significant contribution to the #energy supply with #low-carbon nuclear energy in numerous countries around the world. The company operates to the highest safety standards and closely protects its sensitive technology in a responsible manner.

ETC operates with its group companies also in other market sectors. Pronexos is an ETC venture that provides specialist manufacturing products and services to high-tech industries, with particular focus on carbon fibre products. Stornetic provides high-quality technology solutions in the form of flywheel systems with a focus on sustainable energy supply.

ETC currently employs around 800 people across its five sites in Capenhurst (UK), Jülich (Germany), Almelo (Netherlands), Tricastin (France) and Eunice (USA) and is growing due to increasing global demand for low-carbon energy.

Name: 92 Research - Nuclear Analysis
Description:
92 Research is the research and consulting arm of  the nuclear investment fund, 92 Capital. We provide the technical depth that underpins 92 Capital’s investment strategy while offering advanced engineering, modelling, and analysis services to companies across the nuclear sector.

Our work spans two core areas:
1. Technical due diligence for 92 Capital, including rigorous assessments based on reactor physics, systems engineering, and techno-economics.
2. Consulting and analysis for industry, delivering modelling and simulation, design and systems engineering, regulatory and economic assessments, and support for nuclear project development.

We build modular, open-source driven modelling tools that link reactor physics, thermal-hydraulics, control systems, and techno-economics into a unified workflow. Our expertise includes Monte Carlo methods, fuel behaviour, thermal-hydraulics, optimisation, and MBSE, with experience across advanced reactor concepts (SFR, LFR, MSR, HTGR).

Applications include data-centre power, district heating, marine propulsion, hydrogen production, and other industrial heat and power uses.

92 Research also collaborates with universities and research organisations through grants, postdoc and PhD projects, and European programmes, strengthening both our scientific foundation and the broader research ecosystem.

ETC est une entreprise technologique innovante qui s'engage à fournir à ses clients des services technologiques d'enrichissement et des solutions de haute technologie avancées de manière sûre, compétitive et durable. Nos technologies innovantes contribuent à un

Notre expertise réside dans la conception et la fourniture de solutions de fabrication, d'ingénierie et de technologie hautement qualifiées qui répondent aux besoins des marchés énergétiques mondiaux, aujourd'hui et demain. Avec de nombreuses années d'expérience dans l'industrie nucléaire, les activités d'ETC se concentrent aujourd'hui sur deux domaines clés :
Nous produisons la technologie d'enrichissement de l'uranium par centrifugation gazeuse, la plus efficace au monde pour produire le combustible d'uranium enrichi nécessaire à la production d'énergie nucléaire.  Nous sommes spécialisés dans la fourniture de solutions technologiques innovantes et d'expertise à divers marchés industriels, tels que : le stockage d'énergie par volant d'inertie d'énergie à volant d'inertie, le stockage de l'hydrogène, les produits en fibre de carbone de haute qualité, le soudage,

Name: SASPAM-SA Horizon Euratom Project
Description:
SASPAM-SA (Safety Analysis of SMR with Passive Mitigation strategies - Severe Accident) project proposal has been funded in HORIZON-EURATOM-2021-NRT-01-01, “Safety of operating nuclear power plants and research reactors”.

Key Objective:
-  to investigate the applicability and transfer of the operating large-LWR reactor knowledge and know-how to the near-term deployment of integral PWR  (iPWR), in the view of Severe Accident (SA) and Emergency Planning Zone (EPZ) European licensing analyses needs. 

Key Outcomes:
- To be supportive for the iPWR licensing process by bringing up key elements of the safety demonstration needed;
-  To speed up the licensing and siting process of iPWRs in Europe. 

Key Highlights:
- The applicability of large-LWR reactor knowledge & know-how to the near-term deployment iPWR, in view of SA and EPZ analyses, will be assessed and consolidated. 
- The research priorities will be identified in terms of methodology, code development and experimental needs;
- The knowledge gained  can support Regulators  in decision-making as well as Industry and TSOs in assessing the applicability of iPWR safety features;

The project is coordinated by ENEA and twenty-three Organizations from fourteen Countries are involved: ENEA, CIEMAT, CNRS, EDF, FZJ, GRS, INRNE, IRSN, KIT, KTH, LEI, POLIMI, RATEN, RUB, SINTEC, SSTC-NRS, SURO, TRACTEBEL, TUS, UNIROMA1, VTT, PSI, JRC. 

SASPAM-SA has started on the 1st October 2022 and the planned duration is 48 months; the overall cost is 4276038.85 Euros and the EU Contribution is 2991694.00 Euros. 

Project coordinator is Fulvio Mascari (fulvio.mascari@enea.it)

Funded by the European Union.
Views and opinions expressed are however those of the author(s) only and do not necessarily reflect those of the European Union or European Commission-Euratom.  Neither the European Union nor the granting authority can be held responsible for them.

Name: Advanced Nuclear Fuels Campaign
Description:
The Advanced Fuels Campaign (AFC) is committed to positioning the United States as a global leader in nuclear fuel technology through a robust and forward-thinking strategic vision. Our mission is centered around five pivotal goals:
1. Driving U.S. Leadership in Nuclear Fuel Technology: Leading the development and implementation of state-of-the-art nuclear fuel technologies.
2. Expanding Nuclear Energy Production from the Existing Fleet: Enhancing the efficiency and output of current nuclear reactors.
3. Completing the Qualification Basis for Advanced Reactor Fuel Technology: Establishing the necessary credentials for next-generation reactor fuels.
4. Driving Innovation in Advanced Nuclear Fuel Technology: Fostering groundbreaking research and development in nuclear fuels.
5. Enabling a High-Performing Organization: Ensuring effective program management and proactive stakeholder engagement.

Through these goals, AFC aims to define and implement revolutionary nuclear fuel technologies, support industry progress, achieve critical fuel qualifications, and drive innovative research, all while maintaining high organizational performance. Join us in our mission to advance nuclear fuel technology and secure a sustainable energy future.

Ukrainian Nuclear Society is the largest public nuclear science and technology organization that brings together key enterprises and academic institutions of nuclear industry of Ukraine. Ukrainian Nuclear Society was created in 1993. From that moment, society has been helping to solve a lot of scientific, industry and social problems related to nuclear industry of Ukraine. 

Organization actively cooperates with a lot of industrial companies. The main ranges of work are scientific support of nuclear industry, developing and popularization nuclear safety culture in our country, support for new construction projects for NPP power units, analytics, technical expertise and analytics. We pay great attention to work with public and media, take part in preparation of the future specialists for nuclear industry.

The Ukrainian Nuclear Society actively cooperates with nuclear societies of the world, leading Ukrainian and foreign enterprises of the nuclear industry.

We believe that a clean, carbon-free future is only possible with nuclear energy, which must be actively developed in Ukraine and around the world.

Name: Cambridge Atomworks
Description:
Cambridge Atomworks is a start-up within the Cambridge innovation ecosystem focused on delivering a nuclear micro-reactor built in the UK. We are specialists in nuclear engineering and reactor design, with a mission to redefine energy access by deploying advanced nuclear micro-reactors that deliver clean, reliable power to off-grid industries worldwide. 

Our vision is that by 2030 we will demonstrate the transformative potential of nuclear micro-reactors through an operational prototype. By the mid-2030s, we intend to become a trusted global supplier of clean, sustainable energy to off-grid industries. 

This ambition will be realised through the delivery of the ODIN Micro-reactor, a low-pressure, molten-salt-cooled, solid-fuel fission reactor integrated with power conversion and heat rejection systems, enabling substantial and compact, standalone electricity supply without external connections.

Description:
Bharatiya Nabhikiya Vidyut Nigam Limited (BHAVINI) is a Government Company under the administrative control of the Department of Atomic Energy (DAE) incorporated on 22nd October 2003 as Public Limited Company under the Companies Act, 1956 with the objective of constructing and commissioning the first 500 MWe Fast Breeder Reactor (FBR) at Kalpakkam in Tamilnadu and to pursue construction, commissioning, operation and maintenance of subsequent Fast Breeder Reactors for generation of electricity in pursuance of the schemes and programmes of Government of India under the provisions of the Atomic Energy Act,1962.
BHAVINI is currently constructing a 500MWe Prototype Fast Breeder Reactor (PFBR) at Kalpakkam, 70 Kms away from Chennai. The PFBR is the forerunner of the future Fast Breeder Reactors and is expected to provide energy security to the country. The PFBR is being built with the design and technology developed at the Indira Gandhi Center for Atomic Research (IGCAR) located at Kalpakkam.

Name: ANRIC Enterprises Inc.
Description:
ANRIC is a Global Professional Engineering Firm, established in 1993 to perform technical and professional engineering services associated with the evaluation, development and training in the area of system and component pressure boundary design. 

Our services encompass the power, chemical and petrochemical industries in the nuclear and non-nuclear sectors. ANRIC provides domestic and international clients with services in professional development, quality assurance, engineering and consulting.

ANRIC specializes in providing professional development courses for engineers, managers and stakeholders in the nuclear industry. 

ANRIC is also involved in writing Quality Assurance manuals to CSA Standards and/or ASME Codes, and in assisting companies implement these manuals. In addition, ANRIC undertakes engineering and consulting work, and performs reviews for CRN applications; including acting on behalf of CSA International to issue CRN’s in some jurisdictions.

ANRIC offers a variety of professional development courses encompassing the requirements of the Boiler and Pressure Vessel Codes and Standards as referenced by the Canadian Standards Association (CSA), the American Society of Engineers (ASME) and the International Standards Organization (ISO).

Courses are customized to suit the clients’ needs based on interest, job requirements, and level of expertise.Course instructors at ANRIC are dedicated experts in the nuclear industry and hold key positions on codes and standards committees such as the ASME and CSA. who are employed by or affiliated with regulatory bodies such as the Technical Standards and Safety Authority (TSSA) and the Canadian Nuclear Safety Commission (CNSC).

ANRIC’s Lead Auditors and subject-matter experts are internationally-renowned for their unparalleled practical and theoretical knowledge of Codes and their application gained from years of industry involvement and participation on technical committees that write the Code.

Name: Solid-State Fusion
Description:
Solid State Fusion Energy is a project of the Anthropocene Institute to enable students and investors to make informed opinions on the opportunities and challenges for S-SAFE to address our global energy and climate crisis.

OUR MISSION:

Education & Awareness

- Educate students and professionals on the basics and benefits of Solid-State Atomic and Fusion Energy (S-SAFE) through FAQ, white papers (LENRaries), and articles.
- Evangelize students and professionals to get involved with a call to action (prize to be announced) and events (conference).
- Archive current news from academia and industry, with opinions on how this fits within the field

Momentum & Growth

- To promote and foster excitement of S-SAFE technology in the scientific community.
- Showcase news and media and up to date content about S-SAFE activities
- Add legitimacy and an authoritative voice to S-SAFE community: consistent, forward thinking, and scientifically trusted source.

Connection & Community

- Build bridges between the different S-SAFE groups within the active community
- Act as a hub to the different S-SAFE schools of thought, send traffic to vetted and filtered sites that we view add value to the community
- Resources mapping the space

Description:
Oppenheimer Energy is catalyzing the next generation of nuclear energy projects through advanced financing and delivery models. We develop proven reactor technologies that deliver reliable, carbon-free power and long-term prosperity for communities across the United States.

Our Catalytic Development Model reduces early-stage risk by securing sites, advancing licensing and engineering, and aligning with community and policy goals before utilities and operators join in later project stages. This approach ensures projects move forward with clarity, efficiency, and the “best athlete” partners at every step.

We believe nuclear energy is essential to achieving energy security, climate resilience, and economic abundance. By partnering with utilities, EPC firms, investors, tribal nations, and federal agencies, Oppenheimer Energy is creating the conditions for America’s next gigawatt-scale builds.

Name: Nuclear Utility Obsolescence Group
Description: NUOG's Mission is to create an environment to mutually reduce the impact of obsolescence by identifying beneficial strategic and tactical business practices which may be institutionalized by utilities and suppliers. To create an environment where nuclear utilities and suppliers can mutually identify obsolete items and potential replacements; provide participants with tools that assist in preventing, identifying, prioritizing, and solving obsolescence issues to reduce station risk and vulnerabilities associated with Equipment Reliability. To achieve and maintain a high level of plant operational performance; we must use industry best practices and leverage other Industry Working Groups. Response to obsolescence challenges is provided collaboratively by sharing technical expertise, resources, and operating experience across the industry. We seek out opportunities to work together on initiatives that provide mutual benefit. To align with supplier partners and achieve economies of scale, promote consistency, and to implement best practices. We build on the competence and experience of our suppliers through goal alignment and partnering activities.
