Name: Nuclear Leadership Network
Description:
Welcome to the Nuclear Leadership Network (NLN).

Here you will find all you need to level up your career and arm yourselves with the connection, knowledge and experience to ensure your career is on the best path the Executive Leadership in the UK.

The Nuclear Leadership Newark was established to fill a gap in leadership development. By the time leaders hit a certain level in their path, it becomes more and more difficult to know what development steps to take to ready yourself for a shot at the top executive leadership roles in the Nuclear Industry. As you climb the ladder the number of people available to help you on your path become fewer and fewer, and your journey becomes a much more personal one. There will always be a need to collaborate with others, seek advice and guidance from those around you and take feedback from others seriously, but when you get to a certain point, you realise the more you are on your own.  It’s up to you to develop yourself, through conversations, self reflection, making mistakes and being brave enough to learn from them. Not having all the answers, but being bold enough to seek the answers, is where much of that later growth in leadership occurs.

The Nuclear Leadership Network provides links to the Executive Leaders in the nuclear industry, so you can broaden your access to leaders from outside your own business and from all over the world.

Name: Holtec do Brasil
Description:
A Holtec do Brasil é uma divisão internacional da Holtec International, registrada e atuante no Rio de Janeiro, Brasil. A Holtec do Brasil forma uma parte chave da equipe de execução de projetos da Holtec para o Projeto de Armazenagem a Seco de Angra com a Eletrobrás Eletronuclear, o primeiro do gênero no país. O escopo de trabalho da Holtec do Brasil inclui gerenciamento do projeto, engenharia de projeto, supervisão de fabricação, construção civil (incluindo a operação de um laboratório de testes de materiais na Usina Nuclear de Angra) e serviços. O centro de operações é gerenciado pelo Sr. Luiz Celso, Gerente de Programa. Dr. Rick Springman, Vice Presidente Sênior de Projetos Internacionais, é o executivo da Holtec responsável pelas operações e crescimento da divisão.

A Holtec tem uma longa e excelente reputação de honestidade e integridade nas negociações comerciais. Esta reputação é um bem inestimável que deve ser assegurado continuamente por todos os funcionários da Holtec. Para ter sucesso, a Holtec deve ter a confiança de seus clientes, subcontratados, fornecedores, colegas de trabalho e do público. A manutenção de um compromisso inabalável com os mais altos padrões de ética e conduta comercial é crucial para o futuro da Holtec.

Name: Veracity Nuclear
Description: Veracity Nuclear brings game-changing technology to bear on problems that remain unsolved by traditional modeling and simulation used by the nuclear industry. By integrating automation and design optimization within the high-fidelity capabilities of VERA, analyses may be performed with decreased uncertainties allowing the identification of excess conservatism and the conversion of recovered margins to plant economic benefit.

Name: Exemplary Communications
Description:
As founder of Exemplary Communications, I help nuclear organizations raise performance through clear strategy, leader presence, and workforce alignment. I’m the only communications professional ever to receive the World Association of Nuclear Operators’ Nuclear Excellence Award, with a record of plant performance recoveries and culture change.

I’ve worked at plants, in corporate nuclear HQs, and across INPO/NEI/WANO circles. Strengths: nuclear communications strategy, executive communications, employee engagement, media relations, community trust, and crisis readiness—grounded in technical training from MIT, Westinghouse, and General Physics.

I’m thrilled to add Michael Sexton as our Director of Creative Services—the industry’s premier nuclear videographer/photographer. Together we deliver end-to-end storytelling—from strategy and coaching to on-site production that connects with crews and communities.

And as Michael always says, "A picture is worth a thousand words—and a video is worth a thousand pictures."

Name: nTtau Digital LTD
Description: nTtau Digital is developing the digital design tools to reduce the design time of fusion power plants from years to minutes.  We have established automated simulation workflows that work with parametric CAD and automatic meshing tools.  These are operated within a costing framework developed by Woodruff Scientific with support from ARPA-E that allow fusion power plants to be designed and costed at the same time.  The automated simulation workflows generate training data sets for neural networks which then allow us to perform multi-objective optimization.  It's pretty slick - the digital assets of the optimized system can be viewed on a flat screen or in VR (using @SciVista's SummitVR(TM)).

Name: VietNuc - Vietnam Nuclear Professional Network
Description:
The Vietnam Nuclear Professional Network (VietNuc) unites over 200 highly skilled Vietnamese experts, engineers, professors, researchers, and managers who are making significant contributions to the global nuclear industry. Our members represent leading organizations, including major corporations (EDF, Framatome, ANDRA), prestigious universities and research institutes (CEA, École des Ponts, etc.), nuclear safety regulatory bodies (ASNR), top-tier design and consulting firms (Egis, Tractebel Engineering – ENGIE, GDS, SETEC Nucléaire, etc.), renowned construction giants (BOUYGUES, EIFFAGE, etc.), and key government agencies and initiatives (such as France 2030).

By connecting experts and professionals worldwide in the nuclear field, we play an active role in shaping the future of nuclear energy in Vietnam. Our contributions include providing consultancy in policy, strategy, and project management; participating in seminars, workshops, and training sessions; contributing to education and research at both undergraduate and postgraduate levels; reviewing design and safety documentation; facilitating international partnerships; and engaging directly in the implementation of nuclear energy projects, both locally and internationally.

Join us in advancing the nuclear energy sector and fostering greater collaboration between Vietnam and the global nuclear community.

Name: PULSAR Euratom
Description: PULSAR is a research project funded under the Euratom Research and Training Programme that will develop key enabling technologies for exploration of deep space, where the sun cannot deliver sufficient power to spacecrafts. It will most notably lay the foundations for end-to-end European production of Pu-238, the preferred isotope for fueling space missions, and use a Stirling engine to design a dynamic Radioistope Power System (RPS) that will be up to five times more efficient than the current systems.

Name: Nuclear is Clean Energy Club (NiCE)
Description: NiCE Club @ UC Berkeley is a nuclear advocacy club that runs weekly on Thursdays from 7-8 PM in Etcheverry Hall 4101. We take part in lectures by club members, presentations by guest lectures, and participate in collaborative, immersive post-meeting discussions.

Name: HeatWaveX
Description:
Nuclear Power Plants (NPPs) typically operate on a continuous basis and are designed to provide a stable and reliable source of electricity. However, they may not always be able to respond quickly to changes in electricity demand or supply due to the associated risks and costs.

One potential solution to this challenge is to integrate HeatWaxeX into existing NPPs. HeatWaveX involves the storage of thermal energy in a material that undergoes a phase change, such as melting or solidification. This allows for the storage of large amounts of energy in a relatively small volume, and the release of that energy when needed. The system can be implemented to existing NPPs to achieve flexibility.

The aim is to use the energy stored during periods of low demand to provide additional electricity during high demand periods, while generating profit. This could be accomplished by using HeatWaveX to cool down the steam from Steam Generator (SG) - bypassing it from the turbine - during the low demand, and to preheat the secondary water before it enters the SG - while closing the steam extraction from the turbine - during the high demand. In this way, the NPP can respond quickly to changes in electricity demand without needing to ramp up or down the reactor itself.

Coupling NPPs with HeatWaveX could give an important contribution to address the mismatch between energy production and demand, while generating new revenue for the plant, increasing its overall efficiency and safety, and saving clean water. This innovative approach allows NPPs to effectively adapt to fluctuations in electricity demand without compromising their core operations.

During the last few years, several solutions have been proposed. However, they usually require great land use, expensive technologies, or complex modifications of the power plant circuit. HeatWaveX is overcoming these problems, offering a compact, modular, easy to install and relatively cheap technology which can be produced and sold today!

Name: Nigeria Atomic Energy Commission
Description: Nigeria Atomic Energy Commission is the national focal agency responsible for the development and promotion of peaceful uses of atomic energy and the  National Liaison Office for the International Atomic Energy Agency.

Name: Gibboney Nuclear, PLLC
Description: Gibboney Nuclear, PLLC specializes in licensing consulting for advanced nuclear startups. We are here to guide you through reactor licensing (safety and environmental reviews), regulatory oversight, and constructability reviews.

Name: Rosatom South Asia
Description:
Rosatom South Asia Marketing is a Rosatom regional center in charge of business development, marketing and PR in India, Bangladesh, Sri Lanka, Nepal and Bhutan.
 State Atomic Energy Corporation Rosatom (ROSATOM) is one of global technological leaders, with capabilites in the nuclear sector and beyond, and business partners in over 50 countries. As one of the pioneers of the nuclear industry, ROSATOM has traditionally been at the forefront of the international nuclear market, including nuclear power plant construction, uranium mining and enrichment, and nuclear fuel fabrication and supply. Today, thanks to the unique expertise accumulated over 75 years, the company is conquering the markets of new promising high-tech products. Hydrogen energy, energy storage, nuclear medicine, wind energy, composite materials, logistics business, environmental solutions - in total, more than a hundred new businesses, which cement ROSATOM’s standing among the leading tech giants. The corporation includes about 350 enterprises and organizations employing a total of more than 290,000 people.

Name: Indigo Nuclear
Description: Facilitating the implementation of nuclear power to achieve resilient, decarbonized energy solutions throughout the world.

Name: EUR - European Utility Requirements
Description:
The missions of the EUR Association are to come to common European positions on design requirements to be proposed by Vendors for future Nuclear Power Plants (NPP) and to promote harmonisation in requirements towards standardisation bodies across Europe and worldwide. 

The aims are to ensure their compliance with the safety and technical requirements established by national nuclear authorities, and to facilitate NPP vendors and designs to enter the European market by making the design requirements harmonised and more transparent.

Name: Trident Technology Solutions Group
Description: Inspections, FOSAR, Eddy Current testing, Surface preperation, Coating removal and reaplication.

Name: Nuclear Thought Leaders
Description:
Bringing Nuclear Expertise to the Forefront 
We create and share insightful podcasts and sessions with leading nuclear energy experts, advocating for a sustainable and energy-secure future. Through engaging conversations and expert insights, our platform highlights the critical role of nuclear energy in addressing global challenges like climate change and energy security.

Follow us for thought-provoking content, expert interviews, and discussions that inspire action and innovation in the nuclear industry.

 Let’s connect to drive the conversation forward!

Name: Fusion Advisory Services Ltd
Description: We perform industry-wide surveys in fusion, not only for all of the fusion reactor concepts, but all of the enabling technologies that make them go.  We are a small team, just over 8 of us working from our base at the Harwell Innovation Centre in Oxfordshire, although distributed worldwide.  We work for governments, investors and those in the private fusion industry wishing to understand the market space.

Name: NPHyCo Project
Description:
NPHyCo is an EU research project dedicated to the production of hydrogen from nuclear power. The project is funded by the EU's Euratom Research & Training programme (2021-2025) dedicated to nuclear research and innovation. NPHyco kicked-off in the Autumn of 2022 and will run for two and a half years. 

The EU hopes to fully decarbonise its economy by 2050. One of the solutions being put forward is hydrogen. Nevertheless, to date most of the hydrogen produced in Europe comes from fossil fuels. So the question is how to ramp up the production of low-carbon hydrogen to ensure that enough is being produced – at an affordable cost – to meet the needs of hard to decarbonise sectors, such as industry? 

NPHyCo will focus on the potential for developing large scale, low-carbon, hydrogen production facilities linked to nuclear power plants. It will start by assessing the feasibility of producing hydrogen near an existing nuclear power plant as well as the added value of such project. Furthermore, it will look at potential locations where a pilot project could be implemented.

Name: Clean Energy Frontier
Description:
As we seek innovative solutions to combat climate change, the tri-county region of Bruce, Grey and Huron has an opportunity to build on existing strengths as Canada’s ‘Clean Energy Frontier’.

The truth is the region has what the rest of Ontario needs: expertise and strong local leadership combined with clean, reliable, Ontario-made energy needed to build towards a robust, net-zero economy.

Our message is clear—there’s no path to a net zero future without what happens right here in Bruce, Grey and Huron counties.  

Join us as we tell our story to political, business, and civil society leaders outside the region to build alliances and networks of support.

Name: Korea Atomic Industrial Forum
Description:
Nuclear technology is the most feasible alternative measure to solve the global warming problem as the clean energy which does not emit greenhouse gas and environmental pollution materials. It is used for various industry fields including hydrogen production, desalinization, mass production of radioisotopes for medical uses, development of food & fiber materials using radiation as well as power generation. Also, its scope of practical application is expanding sustainably as the important technology to improve the quality of people's lives. 

KAIF has executed many major businesses including nuclear manpower development, nuclear international cooperation, international seminar, publication of various materials, and public relations business to the nation along with a central roles for the nuclear industry so far since it was founded as the first non-profit organization in October 12, 1972. 

KAIF established the Three Business Visions including promotion between related organizations, building network among domestic and overseas countries, strengthening of educational programs and activities for them in order to play a leading role in the Korean nuclear industry.

Name: EnergoService AD
Description:
The Company provides Engineering and Maintenance Services in Nuclear Automation, Safety, Radiation Protection, Project Management, Instrumentation and Control Systems.
EnergoService carries out maintenance and repair activities of the
Computer-Information and Control System ‘OVATION’ that has been installed in Units 5 and 6 of Kozloduy NPP. In addtion, it serves the
Ventilation Stacks of Radiation Monitoring Systems and Primary Side Chemistry Online Monitoring System of both Units.
*Ovation is a trade mark of Emerson Process Management.
The Company is a partner of Westinghouse Electric Company - a world’s leading provider of safe and innovative nuclear technology, as well as an official distributor for Bulgaria of Mirion Technologies - a global supplier of:
- Health physics products for Nuclear Power Plants, area monitoring and contamination clearance equipment for personnel, tools, clothing,
waste etc.
- Radiation Monitoring Systems
- Neutron Flux Monitoring Systems
EnergoService is a distributor of Hach products in the Nuclear Industry which is a manufacturer of equipment and laboratory instruments for
water measurement and control.

Name: Skills4Nuclear
Description:
The Skills4Nuclear project aims to address workforce and skill shortages in EU nuclear fission and fusion sectors by establishing a long-term collaborative framework that integrates industry, research and training bodies. Its primary goal is to ensure the safe and efficient use of current and future nuclear technologies, including Small Modular Reactors, by fostering industry-driven workforce development and enhancing capacity-building in nuclear safety, waste management, decommissioning, radiation protection and medical applications.

A key project outcome is the creation of a European Forum for Nuclear Workforce and Skills, which will monitor workforce needs, identify emerging skills gaps and continuously update training programs. This forum will serve as a central body ensuring Europe maintains skilled nuclear workforce capable of meeting future challenges. Collaboration of industry and educational institutions will ensure that training curricula are aligned with the latest technological advancements and safety standards.

The project also includes the development of a Nuclear Skills Strategy, a multi-annual roadmap guiding efforts to attract, retrain, upskill and reskill professionals for the nuclear sector. The strategy will promote a fair shift to nuclear energy, prioritizing diversity, inclusion and gender balance. A pilot action based on this strategy will be implemented in Poland.

Another significant outcome is the implementation of reskilling and upskilling programs enabling workers from other industries, such as fossil fuels, to transition into nuclear roles. These programs will prepare the workforce for advanced technologies like SMRs and fusion with a strong focus on safety and innovation.

The project will also develop a series of recommendations and tools aimed at attracting more people to the nuclear sector. This will include broad range of tools which will be tested via a small pilot project and then made available to the wider nuclear communities.

Name: NAYGN OTU
Description:
North American Young Generation in Nuclear (NAYGN) provides opportunities for a young generation of nuclear enthusiasts to develop leadership and professional skills, create life-long connections, engage and inform the public, and inspire today’s nuclear technology professionals to meet the challenges of the 21st century.

The NAYGN - OTU Chapter is the branch representing students, professors, and alumni of ONTechU’s Faculty of Energy Systems and Nuclear Science (FESNS) located in Oshawa, Ontario.

Our Chapter Promotes:
o Professional Development
o Knowledge Transfer and Retention
o Public Information
o Networking & Membership
o Community Outreach

Activities include:

- Hosting events, presentations and discussions with industry professionals

- Site visits to nuclear facilities 

- Speaker presentations by professors contributing to the education of nuclear sciences

- Participation in conferences in conjunction with sister branches, as well as companies & organizations in the nuclear industry such as OPG, AECL, CNS, CNA, etc.

History

NAYGN was established in 1999 and now consists of 100+ chapters across North America. The members of the organization comprise principally students and professionals from the Nuclear Science and Engineering discipline.


OBJECTIVE 
NAYGN’s Mission is to develop leaders to energize the future of nuclear. We provide our members opportunities to focus their energy on improving the nuclear industry and communicating with the public about the benefits of nuclear energy.

We don’t do this alone. NAYGN is a network spanning thousands of young nuclear professionals in North America, each doing their part to make our industry better. As a grassroots volunteer organization, NAYGN members decide and develop the content that NAYGN brings.

Name: Young Nuclear Professionals' Forum (YNPF)
Description: The Young Nuclear Professionals’ Forum (YNPF) is a collective of nuclear professionals early within their career who support the Safety Directors’ Forum (SDF) and Nuclear Engineering Directors’ Forum (NEDF) to improve and promote best practice across the industry by completing task on their or their associated sub-groups' behalf. We consist of representatives from a range of companies in the civil and defence nuclear sector including nuclear licensed sites, MOD nuclear authorised sites, companies with major nuclear interests and regulatory bodies (as agreed by the group membership). The completion of tasks is a key focus of the YNPF and are great for challenging ourselves, gaining experience outside of our day-to-day roles and putting forward an alternative perspective on the challenges facing the nuclear industry today whilst looking to improve the industry for the future.

Name: Emerald Nuclear
Description:

Emerald Nuclear's objective is to become a trusted partner to the maritime industry by delivering state-of-the-art nuclear-electric marine energy and propulsion systems, integrating reactor and power generation technologies seamlessly into vessel infrastructure and onboard systems. Our business model is marine electric energy as a service. 

Emerald Nuclear Norway AS is a wholly-owned subsidiary of Emerald Nuclear Inc. Emerald Nuclear Norway AS serves as the company’s market development and marine engineering entity, with offices located at the Norwegian Maritime Competence Center in Ålesund, Norway. 

Memberships:
We are a member of the Maritime Blue Cluster in Norway: https://www.aakp.no/bluemaritimecluster

Name: Lancs Industries
Description:
Lancs Industries manufactures custom designed radiation shielding products, plastic contaiments such as tents glovebags and sleeving, and protective clothing which increase the safety of workers in potentially hazardous environments. Our products are used to prevent the spread of contamination and reduce exposure to radiation in nuclear power plants, Department of Energy cleanup sites and laboratory facilities, medical facilities and naval shipyards.

With a 28,000 sq. ft. production, warehouse, and administrative facility in Albuquerque NM, Lancs is the largest manufacturer of radiation and nuclear shielding products and protective clothing in North America. Lancs has produced over 80% of the lead blankets in use in nuclear power plants in the United States today. Lancs now carries a ready to ship inventory of many standard radiation shielding products.

Products manufactured by Lancs Industries have a reputation for the highest quality and best value. We meet the most rigorous standards applied in the nuclear operating industry today. Lancs Industries manufactures our products using a flexible design and manufacturing process that is always responsive to customer needs. Material selection, functional design and dimensions can all be uniquely specified by the customer. Lancs will build to order your custom radiation shielding products.

Lancs Industries is a small business enterprise as defined by the U.S. Small Business Administration.

Name: The Splitting Atoms Podcast
Description:
Splitting Atoms shares the story of the Canadian nuclear power industry. Join us on an electrifying journey as we explore the pivotal role that nuclear energy plays in shaping our modern world.

In each episode, we'll dive deep into the heart of the nuclear industry, featuring interviews with some of the most influential minds in the field. Industry leaders will share their expertise, insights, and stories that have shaped the Canadian nuclear landscape.

Splitting Atoms is a blend of education and entertainment designed to increase awareness of this Superhero industry. Discover the incredible ways nuclear power supports our electrified lifestyles, combats the climate crisis, and fuels ground breaking advancements in medical research, particularly in the fight against cancer.

Subscribe to "Splitting Atoms" today and become part of the movement to celebrate the incredible contributions of nuclear power to the world.

At PCE we value safety above all else in order to provide peace of mind to everyone and are committed to the well being of our employees, communities, partners and the environment.

Description: Inspired by the remarkable work of Will Shackel, Founder of Nuclear for Australia, the “Nuclear for BC” initiative was launched by Raphaël Schirrer in October 2023. With the enthusiastic support of his family—including his daughter, who designed the NfBC logo—Raphaël established the initiative to highlight British Columbia's ambiguous stance on nuclear energy, as reflected in the Clean Energy Act of 2010.

The four corner stones of NITSL are Software Quality Assurance, (SQA), Cyber Security, Digital Controls, (DC) and Infrastructure & Applications, (I&A).

Name: Hitachi GE Vernova Nuclear Energy, Ltd.
Description:
From the standpoints of energy security and the prevention of global warming, nuclear power generation is important and will soon embark on a new era. Hitachi-GE Nuclear Energy, Ltd., was established in 2007 by Hitachi, Ltd., and the General Electric Co. of the U.S. as a means of participation in the nuclear energy business.
Hitachi-GE Nuclear Energy, Ltd., having inherited the technologies and the experiences of both companies,each with a half-century of experience in the nuclear energy business, is working for progress in that field while promoting highly reliable manufacturing & services practices.

Name: R&K Anderson Consulting, LLC
Description:
Performance Recovery
We offer assistance in a variety of areas from  evaluations that uncover organizational issues to the corrective actions needed to ensure safe, reliable operations.

Leadership Development
To obtain excellence, an organization needs strong leadership. Based on our experience in helping lead plants to excellence, including five successful regulatory recovery projects, we can provide proven methods to assist your organization in selecting, training and developing strong leaders.

Guest Speakers
Whether in a small classroom or large group setting, we have the insights and experiences of more than 50 years combined nuclear power experience to educate and inspire employees, supervisors, managers and stakeholders.

Name: NuclearNow
Description:
At NuclearNow, we are on a mission to revolutionize the perception of nuclear power. With a dedicated focus on promoting the myriad benefits and advancements within the nuclear industry, we strive to illuminate the path towards a brighter, sustainable future.

Our Approach:

Education and Awareness: We believe that knowledge is power. Through targeted campaigns and informative content, we aim to educate the public about the safety, efficiency, and environmental benefits of nuclear energy.

Community Engagement: Building trust and fostering open dialogue is essential. We engage with communities, stakeholders, and policymakers to address concerns, dispel myths, and highlight the positive impact of nuclear power on local economies and global energy security.

Innovation Showcase: Nuclear technology is continuously evolving. We showcase the latest innovations and breakthroughs in the field, demonstrating how nuclear power is driving innovation, creating jobs, and paving the way for a sustainable energy landscape.

Advocacy and Support: We advocate for policies that recognize the vital role of nuclear power in achieving clean energy goals. By partnering with industry leaders, advocacy groups, and policymakers, we work towards creating a regulatory environment that fosters investment and innovation in nuclear energy.

Global Collaboration: Addressing growing power needs requires a global effort. We collaborate with international partners to share best practices, promote international cooperation, and support the expansion of nuclear energy worldwide.

At NuclearNow, we envision a world where nuclear power is recognized as a cornerstone of clean, reliable energy. Join us in shaping the narrative for a sustainable future powered by nuclear innovation.

Name: SEATOM Technologies
Description:
The International Maritime Organization (IMO) and its member states have set ambitious greenhouse gas emission goals for the next 25 years, aiming for net-zero emissions by 2050. However, given the average lifespan of an ocean-going vessel (approximately 25 years), the absence of a clear, agreed-upon roadmap to achieve these targets poses significant challenges already TODAY.

SEATOM Technologies supports the maritime industry's transition to net-zero by facilitating the adoption of nuclear propulsion for large vessels. The core team began its work well before this mode of power gained traction among shipowners. Since then, SEATOM has assembled a distinguished group of experts, including leaders in nuclear technology, regulatory compliance, naval architecture, and shipbuilding.

Name: AMAG - Advanced Measurement & Analysis Group Inc
Description:
At AMAG, our goal is to provide solutions for owners and operators in the power industry.  

Our products do not violate the pressure boundary since they are non-intrusively installed on pipes, thus eliminating risk of leakages and minimizing costly plant design modifications.

Visit us at www.amag-inc.com

Name: NWS Technologies
Description:
NWS Technologies specializes in the testing, certification, and repair of nuclear safety, relief, and line valves.

In 1996, NWS Technologies began operations with three owners and two employees. We have grown exponentially since then to meet the needs of our customers. We became a single source for both test and repairs services with a focus on customer satisfaction and efficiency. Over the years, our services have expanded to include storage, program development, and technical support.

We continue to focus on serving our customers and we go the extra mile for our customer’s satisfaction. Our work is backed by our guarantee that if any valve fails a test due to our workmanship, it will be repaired and re-tested at no additional cost. This approach has allowed us to become the preferred vendor for the majority of nuclear sites in the United States, as well as select international utilities.

NWS maintains various licenses/programs in relation to its business, including:
• Radioactive Materials License issued through the State of South Carolina
• 10 CFR 50 Appendix B Quality Program
• NBIC – VR and NR Certificate of Authorization
• ASME Section IX Welding Program

We work at the nexus of sustainable nuclear fuel and energy security

Email us at communications [at] alphanur.com

Name: Full Matrix Ltd
Description: Experts in Structural Integrity and Ultrasonic Non-Destructive Testing

Name: DORADO project
Description:
The overall aim of the EU funded project DORADO (Digital twins and Ontology for Robot Assisted Decommissioning Operations) is to improve safety and efficiency in nuclear D&D by applying digital technologies such as Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Building Information Modeling (BIM), using a dedicated decommissioning ontology.

In the DORADO project, a holistic digital platform based on a data-driven approach will be created, integrating digital tools into a coherent suite customized for decommissioning applications. Once completed, the platform can be utilized especially for in situ waste characterization, planning segregation and packing remotely, robotics and remote handling systems, characterization and sampling, clearance of surfaces and structures, cost estimation, risk identification, and knowledge management, including on-site voice assistance to the fieldworkers.

The DORADO project will focus on several technologies that will be developed and integrated to be used with a common data server combining the data flow following the BIM methodology. These include, e.g., point-cloud data, 3D models and change detection, Digital Twins (DTs) based ALARA dose estimation, robot mission optimization, and smart voice assistant interface. After the technological implementation development phase, all the results will be demonstrated with data sets collected from real nuclear facilities.

Main expected impacts are minimizing radiation exposure and risk of occupational incidents to workers, as well as increasing the efficiency of decommissioning planning by enabling remote operations and planning. This can result in significant cost reduction and optimized Waste Management (WM) strategies among others, increasing the social acceptance towards nuclear energy. Utilizing a decommissioning-specific ontology will allow capturing and sharing the complex information involved in D&D projects, enabling better planning, decision-making, and knowledge reuse.

Name: Fermi Development
Description: As the UK advances toward its 2050 net zero carbon emissions target, the role of nuclear energy has become indispensable. Existing and planned nuclear projects only account for 30% of the required 24 GW of nuclear capacity needed to meet this target; the market for new nuclear developments is therefore 17 GW. Fermi Development offers a unique solution to seize this opportunity by integrating modular nuclear technologies with an advanced development strategy to unlock value and reduce risk at each stage of the project development lifecycle.

Name: ASSAS Horizon Europe project
Description:
ASSAS (Artificial intelligence for Simulation of Severe AccidentS) is a Horizon Europe funded project focusing on nuclear severe accident simulators.

The main output of the project is to develop a prototype severe accident simulator based on IRSN’s calculation code ASTEC (Accident Source Term Evaluation Code) and Tecnatom’s simulation suite TEAM_SUITE. It will feature a basic-principle generic pressurized light water reactor (PWR), to be used for phenomenological training. More importantly, it will be a proof-of-concept for the development of more realistic industrial simulators after the end of the project.

One of the challenges of the project is to run the simulator in real time, or even faster, with the lowest possible impact on accuracy. To achieve this goal, different solutions will be explored:
-	Efficient programming techniques,
-	The simplification of reactor models,
-	The development of surrogate models based on machine-learning.
Efforts will concentrate on the last option, which is the most innovative and promising. Various approaches will be tested on ASTEC and the American code Melcor, with different levels of hybridation between AI models and the original code. If successful, the developed methodology may be adapted in the future to other applications in severe accident research and beyond.
 
Partners: IRSN (France), Tecnatom (Spain), CIEMAT (Spain), JSI (Slovenia), KIT (Germany), KTH (Sweden), ENEA (Italy), TU Delft (Netherlands) , Phi-Meca Engineering (France), IVSTT (Slovakia), Energorisk LLC (Ukraine), Bel V (Belgium), CS Group-France (France) and PSI (Switzerland)
Funded by the European Union. Views and opinions expressed are however those of the author(s) only and do not necessarily reflect those of the European Union or European Commission-Euratom. Neither the European Union nor the granting authority can be held responsible for them.

Name: Nordic Nuclear Energy AB
Description:
Nordic Nuclear Energy AB (NNE) is a Nordic nuclear energy company developing next-generation nuclear technologies for a fossil-free and reliable energy system. Building on decades of Nordic experience in reactor design and industrial delivery, the company operates within established regulatory frameworks.

NNE’s primary development and commercial focus is BWR-N, a next-generation Nordic boiling water reactor designed for large-scale, fossil-free baseload power and adapted to Nordic regulatory requirements and industrial needs. In parallel, NNE is developing micro-modular reactor (MMR) solutions for local and industrial applications where smaller, flexible and dispatchable energy production is required.

Name: Progrès Nucléaire
Description: Progrès Nucléaire est une association française dont le but est de promouvoir le progrès pour l’humanité et pour la nature, par l’amélioration des systèmes d’énergie nucléaire.

Description:
Turning vision into results.

At Fusion Enterprises, we provide the strategic foundation for a portfolio of nuclear-focused brands. 

From systems innovation to advanced manufacturing and essential services, we unite great companies and skilled teams to deliver breakthrough results. Our name reflects this fusion of strengths - combining expertise, experience, and integrity to drive process improvement and build lasting trust.

Name: Canadian Nuclear Society - Ontario Tech University
Description:
The Canadian Nuclear Society is a not-for-profit organization serving the nuclear industry representing individuals contributing to, or otherwise supporting nuclear science and engineering in Canada.

The CNS - OTU Branch is the branch representing students, professors, and alumni of OTU’s Faculty of Energy Systems and Nuclear Science (FESNS) located in Oshawa, Ontario.

The branch promotes the exchange of information & knowledge, within the nuclear professional and academic community, in the field of applied nuclear science, engineering, and technology.

Activities include:

- Hosting technical seminars, presentations and discussions from industry professionals

- Site visits to nuclear facilities

- Speaker presentations by professors contributing to the education of nuclear sciences

- Participation in conferences in conjunction with sister branches, as well as companies & organizations in the nuclear industry such as OPG, AECL, NAYGN, CNA, etc.

History

The Canadian Nuclear Society – Ontario Tech University [Formerly UOIT] branch was formed in March of 2005. The members of the CNS - OTU Branch comprise principally of students and professionals from the Nuclear Science and Engineering discipline.


OBJECTIVE 
To organize and facilitate events, activities, and seminars for the exchange of information, fostering the research, development, and beneficial utilization of nuclear science and technology, for peaceful uses.

To act as a not-for-profit corporation in encouraging Education and the exchange of Knowledge in the nuclear industry to enhance professional & technical capabilities of those involved in the nuclear field in the Canadian context.

Name: Nuclear Energy Western Canada
Description:
NEWC is a private-sector developer advancing next-generation nuclear projects in British Columbia and Western Canada.

Our flagship initiative is the province’s first demonstration Small Modular Reactor (SMR) in the Okanagan Valley: a first-mover project designed to anchor regional clean-energy growth and position Western Canada as a leader in advanced nuclear deployment.

THE OPPORTUNITY

BC and Western Canada need dependable, carbon-free power to support industrial growth. Demand is rising across hydrogen and clean-fuel production, mining, O&G, manufacturing, data centres, electrification, and emerging radioisotope applications.
SMRs provide the reliable baseload energy and heat needed to power this future.

WHAT NEWC DELIVERS

• Flagship SMR Project: a scalable, first-of-its-kind site for long-term regional deployment
• GoNuclear! Supply Chain Platform: helping Western Canadian companies enter and compete in the nuclear market
• Indigenous Partnership Model: early, equity-focused collaboration supporting long-term, self-determined economic development
• Government & Regulatory Alignment: preparing policy, permitting, and approvals for SMR deployment
• Industrial Integration: embedding nuclear energy into clean-growth strategies for mining, energy, data centres, hydrogen, and more
• Ecosystem Building: education, workforce pipelines, and public engagement to strengthen nuclear readiness across BC, AB, SK, and the North

WHY NEWC

We combine regional focus, private-sector agility, strategic partnerships, and a clear SMR deployment pathway.
NEWC offers a high-impact entry point into Western Canada’s emerging nuclear economy, supporting clean fuels, industrial electrification including data centres, radioisotopes, and long-term prosperity.

CONTACT US

contact@newc.ca

The conference is Canada’s premier nuclear waste and decommissioning conference and is intended to provide a forum for discussion of the status and proposed future directions of technical, regulatory, environmental, social and economic aspects of radioactive waste management, decommissioning and environmental restoration for nuclear facilities. It will serve to improve communication among specialists, managers, policy makers and other stakeholders in these activities, and to foster international collaboration to benefit society.

The conference is organized into three days of plenary sessions, concurrent technical tracks and poster sessions and there will be a business exhibition to showcase technology, equipment and services. Optional post-conference technical tours to Canadian facilities engaged in nuclear waste management, decommissioning and/or environmental restoration will be held on Thursday, August 31.

Name: #WeAreCriticalMass
Description:
#WeAreCriticalMass is is a social movement inspired by #WeTooCriticalMass, which represents two urgent and related calls to action: (1) growing demand for clean, safe and reliable nuclear energy; and (2) the need for a diverse and inclusive nuclear workforce to meet that demand. 

The concept of “critical mass” was first used in nuclear physics. It represents the minimum amount of a fissionable material that will support a self-sustaining chain reaction. At this mass, the neutrons released in one fission reaction can trigger adjacent atoms to fission. A second definition explains socio-dynamic phenomena that drive actions and behaviors of large segments of the population. Also described as “critical density” in ecology, “threshold models of collective behavior” in sociology, and inflection points for policy influence in gender politics, the term illustrates how a spark ignites social movement. 

Since 2022, responses to surveys conducted by the Pew Research Center have marked a shift in support for nuclear energy in the U.S. with over 50% of adults saying they favor more nuclear generation for electricity. International support is similarly on the rise. In January 2024 World Nuclear News published an article on a global survey that found high public support for nuclear energy. “One-and-a-half times more people support the use of nuclear energy than oppose it.” A social movement is coalescing into an international energy policy direction. We are poised to unlock and mobilize new nuclear technologies that will improve the general welfare of all the world’s people. We are critical mass.

Name: APIS
Description:
APIS is short for Accelerated Program for Implementation of secure VVER fuel Supply.   The objective of the APIS-program is to create security of supply of nuclear fuel for Russian-designed pressurized water reactors (VVER) operating in the EU and Ukraine by diversification of fuel sources in full compliance with nuclear safety. The project is reinforcing European capabilities for supply of VVER-440 type of nuclear fuel by providing Western fuel designs and fabrication capacities as well as by creating cooperation among participating countries in the fuel licensing. The program is co-funded by the European Union through the Horizon program. It was started in January 2023 and has a duration of 36 months. Westinghouse is leading the program, which is supported by twelve different partners from eight countries, including the affected utilities, another fuel manufacturer, fuel engineering, and research organizations

The European partners in APIS from eight countries include utilities: CEZ AS - Czech Republic, Energoatom - Ukraine, Fortum - Finland, MVM Paks Nuclear Power Plant - Hungary, and  Slovenske Elektrarne AS – Slovakia; fuel manufacturers:  Westinghouse (Sweden) and Enusa (Spain);  and fuel engineering-, technical safety-,  and research organizations: JRC-Joint Research Centre-European Commission – Belgium,  State Scientific and Technical Center for Nuclear and Radiation Safety of Ukraine,  UJV REZ AS - Czech Republic,  Uppsala University – Sweden,  and VUJE, a.s., Slovakia.

APIS is Co-funded by the European Union. Views and opinions expressed are however those of the author(s) only and do not necessarily reflect those of the European Union or Euroatom. Neither the European Union nor the granting authority can be held responsible for them

NPO has developed a business model to serve the unique shielding needs of the Nuclear Power industry.  Our Sales and Engineering staff follow a robust process to understand your needs and quickly deliver solutions that work.

NPO products include T-Flex Tungsten, T-Flex Bismuth, Lead Blankets, Lead + Steel fabrications, non-lead, lead, neutron, and magnetic shielding.  Our T-Flex product is flexible and can be manufactured to perfectly fit any component.  Typical T-Flex products include pipe shields, ribbon wrap, valve shields, and magnetic tiles.  NPO utilizes 3D printing technology to create molds that allow T-Flex to embody practically any 3D shape.  

Lead wool blankets are the industry standard radiation shield product for industrial applications.  NPO is the leading manufacturer of lead wool blankets in the world and has been manufacturing them since 1976.

Encasing Lead in steel eliminates its hazards and weak material properties, while retaining its attenuation performance and cost effectiveness.  We take on lead/steel fabrication projects scaling from pounds to tons while achieving some of the most challenging tolerance scenarios.

In addition to nuclear power, NPO also serves the nuclear medicine industry providing Type A shipping containers and other shielding.  NPO collaborates with the radiochemistry experts at sister company, Eichrom Technologies, who work at the forefront of nuclear medicine separation chemistry, to design shielding for this industry.  Our radioactive materials laboratory gives us the ability to test and develop shielding using hot radioactive sources in a radiopharmacy-like environment.

Name: #WeTooCriticalMass
Description:
#WeTooCriticalMass is a social movement, inspired by the #MeToo movement that swept the world last decade. Our movement represents two urgent and related calls to action: (1) growing demand for clean, safe and reliable nuclear energy; and (2) the increasing demand for a diverse nuclear workforce that is welcoming to and representative of societies and populations across the globe. 

The concept of “critical mass” was first used to describe a phenomenon in nuclear physics. Ubiquitous in the nuclear industry, the term refers to the minimum amount of a fissionable material that will support a self-sustaining chain reaction. At this mass, the neutrons released in one fission reaction can trigger adjacent atoms to fission. British science historian Margaret Gowing is credited with coining the term around 1940.

“Critical mass” also is used to explain socio-dynamic phenomena that drive actions and behaviors of large segments of the population. Also described as “critical density” in ecology, “threshold models of collective behavior” in sociology, and inflection points for policy influence in gender politics, the term illustrates how a spark ignites social movement. 

In 2016 Susan Fowler, then a software engineer at Uber, sparked a conversation that ignited social movement after she encountered sex discrimination, reported it, and suffered retaliation. Uber threw away her talent. On February 19, 2017, Fowler turned whistleblower in a courageous blog post that went viral, launching the #MeToo movement.

This decade brings a new social movement and growing demand for clean, reliable and safe energy. The solution is nuclear. A trained and skilled workforce is needed to deliver this solution. However, today’s workforce comprises a large retirement-eligible demographic. 

The nuclear future cannot afford to discourage, exclude and lose talent. Women in STEM fields and skilled trades will be vitally important for humankind to realize nuclear solutions to pressing challenges.

Name: Cortex Fusion Systems, Inc.
Description:
Cortex Fusion Systems is developing compact fusion reactors that use ultrafast lasers and nanophotonics to achieve fusion through self-generated magnetic confinement and isochoric bulk heating.
Our patented approach uses femtosecond laser pulses carrying orbital angular momentum to drive electrons into azimuthal currents that generate solenoidal magnetic fields exceeding 1000 Tesla. At these field strengths, hot electrons are confined on micron-scale gyro-orbits, keeping them localized within the interaction volume long enough to collisionally transfer their energy to ions and reach thermonuclear temperatures. Nanophotonic targets waveguide the laser into dense matter at constant volume, enabling isochoric heating that bypasses the hydrodynamic instabilities and ablative compression of conventional inertial confinement fusion.
The fuel is a continuously flowing room-temperature liquid jet containing nanostructured fusion fuel. Because the target replenishes itself between shots, we operate at thousand+ pulses per second rather than a few shots per day. This high repetition rate is what makes continuous power generation possible and distinguishes our approach from single-shot inertial fusion schemes. Liquid jet delivery also eliminates the cryogenic pellet fabrication and injection systems that have bottlenecked other laser fusion efforts.
Our goal is modular power blocks that scale through repetition rate and arraying rather than facility size. We are currently building the first electricity-producing fusion reactor, with plans to deploy compact power plants across the United States and internationally.
Founded 2021 in New York. Venture-backed. Four non-provisional patents.
If you work in ultrafast laser engineering, plasma physics, or energy systems investment, connect with us or visit cortexfusion.systems.

Adaptive Energy Systems is redefining the future of energy with groundbreaking technologies like Liquid Fluoride Thorium Reactors (LFTRs), Small Modular Reactors (SMRs), and molten salt systems. Focused on delivering sustainable, safe, and efficient energy solutions, our innovations are designed to address the pressing need for clean electricity and hydrogen production in a rapidly evolving energy landscape.

At the heart of our approach is LFTR SMR technology, which utilizes molten salt as a coolant and fuel solvent, ensuring safe, low-pressure operations while achieving exceptional thermal efficiency. By employing thorium, an abundant and inherently safe fuel source, our reactors significantly reduce long-lived radioactive waste, offering a cleaner and more sustainable alternative to traditional nuclear power systems. These reactors are compact, versatile, and scalable, making them ideal for addressing energy needs across diverse industries and regions.

Our reactors are not just about electricity—they also integrate hydrogen production capabilities through high-temperature electrolysis, making them a vital component in the transition to a hydrogen-based economy. This dual-purpose approach ensures that we contribute to decarbonizing multiple sectors, from power and transportation to heavy industry, positioning hydrogen as a clean, reliable energy carrier for the future.

Our vision is to lead the charge in creating a resilient and sustainable energy infrastructure that meets the demands of today while preparing for the challenges of tomorrow. Through innovation, adaptability, and dedication, we aim to redefine global energy solutions and leave a lasting positive impact on the environment and future generations. Join us as we drive the energy revolution forward.

Name: Solid Atomic
Description:
Solid Atomic is the premier developer of commercial use Solid State atomic power reactor technology.  We specialize in photon intermediate direct energy conversion know as PIDEC.  PIDEC reactor technology represents the most efficient fission to electricity conversion cycle at a fraction of the cost of traditional Carnot Nuclear energy.  

PIDEC technology works by pairing the fission fuel with both an excimer and a photovoltaic both designed to withstand the punishing environment inside of a nuclear reactor.

PIDEC technology isn't new, it was developed over 40 years ago by Mark Prelas Ph.D.  This technology was tested on the MURR research reactor at the University of Missouri.  

We are applying state of the art materials technology to bring the best kept secret in atomic power technology to the main stage as a high power density zero-carbon source of clean energy.
